comments.reader: Asel has achieved the age-related standard in reading. Reading every day from engaging books and actively discussing what she reads will be essential for Asel's continued progress in Year 5.

Building her vocabulary choices will also help her express her ideas more precisely. Drawing more actively on her reading for inspiration and language will support her development as a writer. With continued focus on connecting her ideas clearly within and across sentences, Asel will be able to show more of her potential in Year 5.

Arthur chooses from a wide range of texts and talks about his reading with genuine interest. The key areas for Arthur to develop further are his comprehension skills — particularly inference, where he is growing in his ability to draw conclusions about characters' feelings and motives from text evidence, and retrieval, where he is building confidence in locating and recording information from texts. Arthur's reading vocabulary is developing well and beginning to enrich his written work. Continuing to focus on deeper comprehension skills through guided reading and discussion will support his progress in Year 5.
comments.writer: Arthur has achieved the age-related standard in writing. He writes with accuracy and confidence, showing strong technical skills across spelling, punctuation and grammar. Arthur uses inverted commas for direct speech accurately and applies fronted adverbials with commas effectively in his writing. His handwriting is fluent and legible, supporting the presentation of his ideas. Arthur makes thoughtful vocabulary choices and his non-fiction writing is particularly strong, showing clear structure and purpose. The main area for Arthur to develop is his composition in fiction writing, where he is growing in his ability to develop characters and narrative voice. Drawing more actively on his wide reading to inform his creative writing will be an important focus for Year 5, building on the strong technical foundations he has established.
comments.mathematician: Arthur has achieved the age-related standard in mathematics and in some aspects has been learning at greater depth. He is a capable and confident mathematician who applies himself consistently across all areas of the curriculum. Arthur's excellent fluency with multiplication tables underpins his strong work on fractions, decimals and division, and his written calculation methods are accurate and efficient. He tackles mathematical concepts with confidence and shows good understanding of place value and number relationships. The areas for Arthur to develop further are his mathematical reasoning and problem-solving skills, where he is building confidence in explaining his thinking and tackling more open-ended challenges. Continuing to deepen his ability to communicate mathematical ideas and approach unfamiliar problems systematically will be key focuses in Year 5.
comments.learner_21c: Arthur, you have shown excellent progress as a 21st Century Learner throughout Year 4 and your enthusiasm for learning is wonderful to see. Your curiosity drives you to ask thoughtful questions and explore ideas further, and your resilience when facing challenges is impressive — you do not give up easily and this determination serves you well. You work very independently and collaborate effectively with others, showing real respect for different perspectives. Your effort and commitment are consistent and noticed every day. The area for you to develop further is your oracy — growing in confidence to contribute more frequently in whole-class discussions and sharing your ideas more openly. Keep embracing challenges with the same enthusiasm in Year 5, Arthur.
comments.rights: Arthur, you have an excellent understanding of your rights and responsibilities as a learner and this shows consistently in the Maple Learning Zone. You listen with genuine respect to others and contribute thoughtfully when you share your ideas. Your commitment to your own learning and support for others around you creates a positive learning environment for everyone. You approach every challenge with determination and show real pride in the quality of your learning. These qualities will serve you very well in Year 5.
